item.title: Junkanoo Fire, Nursing Scandal, Minister Backed
item.ns0:title: Junkanoo Fire, Nursing Scandal, Minister Backed
item.description:
Tonight on Our News Weekend, investigators are on the scene at Junkanoo Beach following a late-night structural fire at the popular destination, while Progressive Liberal Party MPs rally behind Finance Minister Michael Halkitis amid growing calls for his resignation. We dive into the controversy surrounding a Grand Bahama nursing program as students and administrators speak out, and bring you the details of a major drug crackdown resulting in multiple arrests across the northern islands. Our team also examines the potential threat of international tariffs on the local fishing industry and takes you to the heart of the 37th annual Pineapple Festival in Eleuthera. Plus, stay tuned for the latest highlights from the sports world and a full tropical weather outlook as a new moisture plume approaches the islands.
-----
Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.
item.ns1:encoded:
Tonight on Our News Weekend, investigators are on the scene at Junkanoo Beach following a late-night structural fire at the popular destination, while Progressive Liberal Party MPs rally behind Finance Minister Michael Halkitis amid growing calls for his resignation. We dive into the controversy surrounding a Grand Bahama nursing program as students and administrators speak out, and bring you the details of a major drug crackdown resulting in multiple arrests across the northern islands. Our team also examines the potential threat of international tariffs on the local fishing industry and takes you to the heart of the 37th annual Pineapple Festival in Eleuthera. Plus, stay tuned for the latest highlights from the sports world and a full tropical weather outlook as a new moisture plume approaches the islands.
-----
Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.
item.ns0:summary:
Tonight on Our News Weekend, investigators are on the scene at Junkanoo Beach following a late-night structural fire at the popular destination, while Progressive Liberal Party MPs rally behind Finance Minister Michael Halkitis amid growing calls for his resignation. We dive into the controversy surrounding a Grand Bahama nursing program as students and administrators speak out, and bring you the details of a major drug crackdown resulting in multiple arrests across the northern islands. Our team also examines the potential threat of international tariffs on the local fishing industry and takes you to the heart of the 37th annual Pineapple Festival in Eleuthera. Plus, stay tuned for the latest highlights from the sports world and a full tropical weather outlook as a new moisture plume approaches the islands.
-----

item.ns0:author: Our News Bahamas
